ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Petition To Terminate Special Needs Trust
Off Calendar Sep-19-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Arthur Park for Trustee. William Ashley for Penny Bernard.

Mr. Ashley presented a copy of a dismissal without prejudice of the Petition for Continuation of Special Needs Trust, etc. which was filed today. Mr. Park expressed concern about the matter being dismissed "without prejudice". Mr. Ashley stated that Ms. Bernard wanted to preserve her claim. The Court granted the Petition to Terminate Special Needs Trust and Discharge Trustee (subject to receipt and review of the order).

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. McConico. (Not Reported)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Petition To Terminate Special Needs Trust
Off Calendar Jun-17-2014 Continued To Sep-09-14 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Arthur Park for Nick Tarlson. William Ashley appeared by telephone for Penny Bernard.

10:55 The matter was called. The Court noted that Ms. Bernard was not present. Mr. Ashley stated that Ms. Bernard should be arriving any moment. The Court took a recess. 11:08 The matter is re-called. Ms. Bernard is still not present. Mr. Ashley stated that he received a call from Ms. Bernard indicating that she was told by Mr. Park's office not to appear. Mr. Ashley stated that Ms. Bernard is not coming. The Court noted that the matter has been continued 12 times, that the Probate Examiner specified problems with the petition, that today an amended petition, memorandum of points and authorities, and a declaration appeared on the Court's register of actions and that is did not see a proof of service. Mr. Ashley explained that file and serve express was to do a proof of service, expressed his concerns about Ms. Bernard's needs, explained his medical issues and financial issues, his inability to continue representation of Ms. Bernard, and argued that the Trustee did not perform his duties as Trustee. Mr. Park asked that his Client be released as Trustee and stated that an accounting had been provided. Further oral argument was presented. The Court raised the issue the remainder beneficiaries, the 30-day notice requirement of the amended petition and filing of a substitution of attorney. The Petition to Terminate Special Needs Trust and the Petition for Continuation of Special Needs Trust, etc. were continued to September 9, 2014 at 10:30 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of a substitution of attorney.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Court Reporter: Denise Doucette, CSR#5963 (707) 246-3323. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Allen. (Reported)

Petition For Continuation Of Special Needs Trust, Recovery Of Trust Property, Reduction And Recoupment Of Compensation Withdrawn By Trustee And Formal Account
Off Calendar Jun-17-2014 Continued To Sep-09-14 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Continuation Of Special Needs Trust, Recovery Of Trust Property, Reduction And Recoupment Of Compensation Withdrawn By Trustee And Formal Account
Off Calendar Apr-15-2014 Continued To Jun-17-14 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Arthur Park for Nick Tarlson. William Ashley for and with Penny Bernard.

Mr. Ashley stated that Ms. Bernard's family trust has 5 subsidiary trusts, that there is over $1,000,000.00 in the family trusts, that Ms. Bernard is a beneficiary under a numerous of said trusts, that she has one sibling who was a beneficiary and trustee, that the sibling was removed from the Special Needs Trust ("SNT"), that the SNT was originally funded for $300,000.00 and that it was inexplicably reduced to $100,000.00. Mr. Ashley also stated that the Attorney who drafted the SNT also drafted the family trust, that he (Mr. Ashley) believes the Settlors' intent was to support Ms. Bernard's needs for the rest of her life, that he believes the SNT Trustee should put a demand on the Sister, suggested mediation, stated that he would like to have someone come in and that he will continue to assist, and explained personal reasons/circumstances as to why he needs to step out. Mr. Park stated that the SNT had $100,000.00, that the money ran out, that Mr. Tarlson is not interested in mediation and requested that the Petition to Terminate Special Needs Trust be added back to the calendar. The Petition for Continuation of Special Needs Trust, etc. was continued to June 17, 2014 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for procedural issues to be addressed.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Court Reporter: Angie Diner, CSR 581 (415) 551-0332. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Allen. (Reported)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Funding Of The Trust
Off Calendar Apr-29-2013 Continued To Jul-17-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Arthur Park for Nick Tarlson. William Ashley for and with Penny Benard.

Mr. Ashley reported that there is no funding of the Trust and that he has been in negotiation with the Trustees to find a source of funding. Mr. Park informed the Court that Mr. Tarlson received funds from Revenue fund recovery back in October and that Mr. Tarlson wishes to resign as Trustee pending appointment of a new Trustee. Status on funding of the trust was continued to July 17, 2013 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 (same date of hearing on Petition for Recovery of Trust Property) to see how efforts of negotiation with the Trustee are going. The Court suggest Counsel meet and confer.

Judge: Lillian K. Sing. Clerk: Nancy Bautista/Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy M. Benton.

Petition To Terminate Trust And For Discharge Of Surety Bond.
Off Calendar Feb-01-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Arthur Park for and with Nick Tarlson (Trustee). William Ashley for and with Penny Bernard (Beneficiary/Objector).

Ms. Bernard requested special accommodations which have been provided. The Court Reporter enlarged the font so that Ms. Bernard could view the screen. Mr. Ashley represented to the Court that the equipment is satisfactory and that Ms. Bernard can follow the proceeding. The Court informed Mr. Ashley that all Parties remaining in the Courtroom are Court staff. All Parties presented oral argument to support their positions as to why the Trust should/should not be terminated, provided trust history and explained circumstances. The Court explained that the only reason the Trust is before the Court today is because of the bond issue, that the Family Trust is not before the Court and also pointed out that the objections are not verified. The Petition to Terminate Trust and For Discharge of Surety Bond was taken under submission.

Judge: Lillian Sing. Court Reporter: Rhonda Aquilina, CSR 956 (415)-551-0629. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy Henry.

Petition For Transfer Of Venue
Off Calendar Jul-17-2007 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Andrew Dimitriou appearing for petitioner. Michael Stecher with co counsel Julie Chan appearing for respondent.
The court is informed that a settlement has been reached. The parties prefer that the settlement not go on record. Nick Tarseck is the successor trustee. A copy of the proof of service is presented to the court. A bond in the amount of $105,000.00 is required. The following orders have been signed: 1)Order Appointing Successor Trustee 2)Order To Deliver Funds (being held by the clerk until the bond is filed) 3)Order Dismissing Petition
